application.purpose: Oracle APEX application generated from SQL exports under the apex/ folder.
application.business_problem: Replace this with the real business problem solved by the application.
application.scope: This documentation is generated during the deployment pipeline from every .sql export found under the apex/ folder.
limitations: Only metadata present in the APEX SQL export can be extracted automatically.
Database objects deployed outside the APEX export may appear only as referenced tables or objects.
Screenshots, exact business process wording, external API contracts, and environment-specific infrastructure notes should be maintained manually when required.

architecture.frontend: Oracle APEX browser-based web user interface
architecture.backend: Oracle APEX runtime, session state, page processes, validations, dynamic actions, SQL, PL/SQL, and built-in APEX APIs
architecture.cloud_infrastructure: ORDS/APEX runtime environment configured for the target deployment

api.authentication: Use the extracted APEX/ORDS authentication method when present. Update manually for APIs outside the APEX export.
api.rate_limits: Not defined in the APEX SQL export. Define ORDS/API Gateway/service-level limits if applicable.

security.authentication_method: Use extracted APEX authentication schemes when present. Update manually for SSO/custom authentication.
security.encryption_details: Use HTTPS/TLS in transit. Database encryption depends on the configured Oracle Database/OCI environment.
security.audit_logging: Review APEX activity logs, custom audit tables, and database auditing configuration.
best_practices: Store secrets only in approved secret stores, never in source control.
Apply authorization schemes to sensitive pages, buttons, processes, and APIs.
Validate input server-side and avoid leaking ORA-/ERR- details to end users.
Use least privilege for database schemas and service users.
Review audit logs for authentication, authorization, data changes, and administrative actions.

faq.question: How do I access the application?
faq.answer: Open the environment-specific APEX URL and sign in with an authorized account.

faq.question: What should I do if I see an error?
faq.answer: Capture the page name, timestamp, screenshot, and steps taken, then contact the application administrator.

troubleshooting: Confirm that all required fields are completed.
Refresh the page and retry the action once.
If the problem persists, share the page name, input values, timestamp, and screenshot with support.
